title: T-128 — Schema Shadow Preview screen (SET-034)
prompt:
# T-128 — Schema Shadow Preview screen (SET-034)

## Goal
Build SET-034 Schema Shadow Preview as a spec-driven Settings screen for dry-running a draft schema column in a simulated form with sample data and no production writes.

Canonical Settings prototype source of truth is `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx`. No exact canonical prototype exists for SET-034, so implement from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md SET-034` and PRD §6.6.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/schema/preview/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/schema/preview/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given `/settings/schema/preview` renders, then it follows UX SET-034: 40/60 split, draft column selector on the left, rendered sample React form on the right, and a persistent preview-only notice.
- Given a draft column is selected, when preview renders, then it uses generated runtime schema/sample data without writing to production schema, reference tables, or tenant migrations.
- Given Publish this Column is clicked, when the user has permission, then it routes/calls the same Step 8 publish path used by SET-031/T-097 and preserves concurrent-edit handling.
- Given no drafts, loading, permission-denied, or schema-generation error states occur, then the screen renders clear non-mutating states.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL page tests for split layout, draft selection, sample form render, preview-only notice, publish handoff, no-draft/loading/error/permission states.
- GREEN: build page wired to existing schema draft/runtime helpers.
- Run page test, typecheck, lint, and capture UI evidence per policy.

## Risk red lines
- Do not create an ad-hoc schema editor or duplicate SET-031 publish logic.
- Do not write production schema, migrations, or reference data from preview render.
- Do not claim 1:1 prototype parity; exact canonical prototype is missing and UX SET-034 is authoritative.
- Do not bypass L1/L2/L3 promotion and concurrent-edit rules.
## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED -> GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

prd_refs: §6.4 Draft / Publish model shadow tenant preview
pipeline_inputs.description: SET-034 Schema Shadow Preview production screen (spec-driven; no exact prototype).
pipeline_inputs.details: Spec-driven UI: no exact canonical prototype exists under `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx` for SET-034. `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md SET-034` and PRD §6.4/§6.6 are authoritative. Preview is non-mutating and reuses existing schema draft/runtime/publish helpers rather than duplicating the column wizard.
out_of_scope: create a new schema publish flow
acceptance_criteria: Schema Shadow Preview renders UX SET-034 split layout with draft selector, rendered sample form and preview-only notice.
Selecting a draft column renders generated runtime schema/sample data without production writes.
Publish this Column hands off to the existing SET-031/T-097 Step 8 publish path with concurrent-edit handling.
No-draft, loading, permission-denied and schema-generation error states are non-mutating and covered by tests.

UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

GREEN: build page wired to existing schema draft/runtime helpers.
Run page test, typecheck and lint.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
